items.title: Poilievre holds news conference about Liberals' fiscal approach
items.summary_auto: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre holds a news conference in Toronto about the federal government’s spending.

items.title: Rally held to demand answers about Jack and Lilly Sullivan disappearance in N.S.
items.summary_auto: Under a cloudless sky in central Nova Scotia, about 50 people gathered outside an RCMP detachment Saturday to demand answers about the disappearance of two young children exactly one year ago.

items.title: This N.L. pharmacist lost a ring 20 years ago. An Ontario man found it in a pair of jeans
items.summary_auto: After finding a golden ring in a thrifted pair of jeans in Ontario, Dan Howard spent 15 years tracking down its bearer. The owner, pharmacist Bradley Buckle, turned out to be half the country away in St. John's.

items.title: Mother of boy who died in care of Ontario couple speaks for 1st time ahead of trial decision
items.summary_auto: In 2021, a 12-year-old died while in the care of a Burlington, Ont., couple who are now charged with his murder. For the first time, the boy's mother is sharing her perspective with CBC Hamilton, saying she wants people to understand that her family is "not just a case or story."

items.title: NASA says it will put humans on the surface of the moon in 2028. How realistic is that?
items.summary_auto: The Artemis II lunar mission is over, and NASA is already looking ahead, planning to put astronauts on the surface of the moon by 2028. But there are a lot of hurdles that need to be cleared before that happens.

items.title: In Pompeii, ancient graffiti depicting daily life is being revealed thanks to modern technology
items.summary_auto: The walls of a corridor that once linked two theatres in the ancient Roman city of Pompeii hold hundreds of inscriptions made by residents before Mount Vesuvius erupted almost 2,000 years ago. An international research team is now using a very modern technique to bring them to life.

items.title: Jeff Bezos to Beyoncé: What to know ahead of the 2026 Met Gala
items.summary_auto: One of New York's hottest events is under pressure this year — not from declining attendance, glamour or budget — but from criticism, bad press and even calls for a boycott.

items.title: Canada's new sovereign wealth fund: What Ottawa can learn from Alberta
items.summary_auto: Alberta launched its sovereign wealth fund decades ago, but it was regularly poached by politicians, which is why the bank balance failed to meaningfully grow.

items.title: Amid growing calls to limit teens' use of AI chatbots, are parental controls enough?
items.summary_auto: Concerns are growing about how some young people engage with AI chatbots. Meta is rolling out a tool to let parents monitor topics their children discuss at the same time as Manitoba is proposing banning use of AI chatbots for youth. More research is needed, but evidence suggests some risk factors for mental health.

items.title: Youth knee surgeries are rising in Quebec. Here's how to prevent them
items.summary_auto: The number of patients under 18 years old who have undergone anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) repair surgery is on the rise in the province. A Montreal orthopedic surgeon says it’s because sports are getting more competitive.

items.title: I didn't want to become my alcoholic father. I know, for better or worse, I never will be
items.summary_auto: Fernanda Rodriguez got a shocking call from her father when she was just nine — a call that opened her eyes to the many challenges her father faced when it came to alcoholism and depression. As she grew older, she was able to see that he persevered through difficulties to be present for her as much as he could be.

items.title: As Carney visits Armenia, experts urge deeper Canada ties in the Caucasus
items.summary_auto: Mark Carney is expected to spend the bulk of his few hours in Armenia engaging with European leaders, but experts say he has momentum to build ties with Yerevan and the rest of the Caucasus as well, something first undertaken by Trudeau.

items.title: Summer McIntosh sizzles at Florida meet with pair of freestyle victories
items.summary_auto: For as dominant as Canadian swimming superstar Summer McIntosh has been for the last number of years, there’s no sign of complacency or let up from the 19-year-old from Toronto.

items.title: Cherie DeVaux becomes 1st female trainer to win Kentucky Derby as Golden Tempo takes 152nd edition
items.summary_auto: Cherie DeVaux made history on Saturday as the first woman to train the winner of the Kentucky Derby, as Golden Tempo charged from the back of the pack and charged down the stretch to get to the finish line first in the biggest event in horse racing.

items.title: Two Canadians on aid boats intercepted by Israel have been released, organizers say
items.summary_auto: A spokesperson for a group trying to break the Israeli navy blockade of Gaza to deliver aid says two Canadians who were detained by Israel in the Mediterranean Sea have been released.
